title: 12. Webcam and Heart Rate
description: How presence-style sensory sources should be used carefully, with clear consent and conservative behavior rules.

steps.title: Presence Sources
steps.body: Webcam and Heart Rate are more personal than screen or clipboard. Enable them only when the user wants this kind of awareness. Use Read steps aloud or Read Step in this tutorial panel if you want spoken guidance while you work.

steps.title: Webcam Context
steps.body: Webcam is useful for posture, gaze, expression, visible props, and presence cues. Use the Source Overview and Source Setup cards to keep the capture purpose clear, and react only to visible evidence.

steps.title: Heart Rate Context
steps.body: Heart Rate reacts to BPM states instead of images. Its source cards should explain current state, thresholds, and optional pulse rules so reactions are tied to measurable changes.

steps.title: Avoid Overreach
steps.body: Presence sources should be conservative. Prefer simple observations and opt-in reactions over confident claims about mood, health, or intent.
